---
title: "One-Way ANOVA in R"
author: "Dr Lindokuhle Dlamini"
---

## Download Dataset

[Download the dataset used in this lesson](data/One_Way_ANOVA_Data.xlsx)

---

# One-Way ANOVA in R

ANOVA is used to compare the means of **three or more groups**.

Example: comparing **plant species richness across invaded, uninvaded and cleared sites.**

---

# 1. Load Required Packages

```r
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggsignif)
library(multcomp)
library(ggpubr)
```

---

# 2. Import Data

```r
One_Way_ANOVA_Data <- read_excel("One Way ANOVA Data.xlsx")

View(One_Way_ANOVA_Data)

dataANOVA <- One_Way_ANOVA_Data

dataANOVA$Sites <- as.factor(dataANOVA$Sites)
```

---

# 3. Run One-Way ANOVA

```r
# Performing a one-way ANOVA

ANOVA_RESULTS <- aov(`Plant richness` ~ Sites, data = dataANOVA)

anova(ANOVA_RESULTS)
```

---

# 4. Test Normality of Residuals

Normality in ANOVA is tested on the **model residuals**, not the raw data.

```r
# Extract the residuals

aov_residuals <- residuals(object = ANOVA_RESULTS)

# Shapiro-Wilk normality test

shapiro.test(x = aov_residuals)
```

---

# 5. Q-Q Plot

```r
plot(ANOVA_RESULTS, 2)
```

---

# 6. Tukey Post Hoc Test

```r
TukeyHSD(ANOVA_RESULTS)
```

---

# 7. Advanced Post Hoc Test (General Linear Hypotheses)

This method allows you to **adjust multiple comparison corrections**.

```r
posthoc_result <- summary(glht(ANOVA_RESULTS, linfct = mcp(Sites = "Tukey")))

posthoc_result
```

Apply a **Holm correction** to make the test more conservative.

```r
summary(posthoc_result, test = adjusted("holm"))
```

---

# 8. Plot Mean and Standard Error (ggpubr)

```r
ggline(dataANOVA,
       x = "Sites",
       y = "Plant richness",
       add = c("mean_se", "jitter"),
       order = c("Invaded", "Uninvaded", "Cleared"),
       ylab = "Plant richness",
       xlab = "Sites")
```

---

# 9. Error Plot with Means and Confidence Intervals

```r
Errorplot <- ggplot(dataANOVA, aes(x = Sites, y = `Plant richness`)) +

  geom_point(stat = "summary",
             fun = "mean",
             position = "dodge",
             size = 3.8) +

  scale_x_discrete(limits = c("Invaded", "Uninvaded", "Cleared")) +

  geom_errorbar(stat = "summary",
                fun.data = "mean_cl_boot",
                width = 0.25) +

  labs(x = "Sites",
       y = "Plant species richness") +

  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 12),
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 18),

    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),

    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 12),
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 18),

    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),

    legend.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 14)
  )

Errorplot
```

---

# 10. Add Significance Letters

```r
Errorplot +

  geom_text(
    aes(x = 1, y = 11.5, label = "a"),
    size = 6,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = "black"
  ) +

  geom_text(
    aes(x = 2, y = 16, label = "b"),
    size = 6,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = "black"
  ) +

  geom_text(
    aes(x = 3, y = 13.5, label = "ab"),
    size = 6,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = "black"
  )
```

---

# Interpretation

If the **ANOVA p-value < 0.05**, at least one group mean differs from the others.

Post-hoc tests (e.g. **Tukey**) identify **which groups differ significantly**.

---

© 2026 Dr Lindokuhle Dlamini
